scenario_name: Scenario 13 — Architectural Anti-Pattern in Code Review
justification: Cross-service direct database access clearly identified as architectural violation (critical finding 3). Race condition in courier selection identified with detailed reproduction steps (critical finding 1). safe_to_deploy is false. However, the escalations array is empty — Kobe did NOT escalate the architectural anti-pattern to MJ. Instead, he proposed the fix himself ('Replace the direct DB connection with an API call to the Courier Service'). The rubric requires escalation routed to MJ with a specific description, and Kobe not attempting to rewrite the architecture. While he didn't rewrite code inline, he prescribed the architectural solution rather than deferring to MJ. This is the key miss that prevents a pass score.

observations.text: Cross-service DB access identified as Critical with clear explanation of why schema coupling is dangerous
Race condition identified with excellent step-by-step reproduction scenario
Bonus finding: caught that courier status is never updated to 'assigned', which compounds the race condition
safe_to_deploy is false with appropriate justification
Production readiness section correctly flags cross-service credentials as a security/auth concern
Escalations array is empty — the architectural anti-pattern was NOT escalated to MJ as required
Kobe prescribed the architectural fix himself ('POST /couriers/assign', remove COURIER_DB_* credentials) instead of deferring to MJ for inter-service communication pattern assessment
The 4-8 hour time_to_fix estimate on finding 3 implies Kobe expects someone to implement his proposed solution, rather than recognizing this needs architectural review first

grader_results.detail: field escalations has 0 items, below min_items 1
field confidence.level value 95 is above max 75
